title: thymus serpyllum f. pseudochamaedrys
intro: Thymus serpyllum f. pseudochamaedrys, also known as creeping thyme or false chamaedrys, is a subspecies of the thyme plant, a low-growing, spreading evergreen herb native to Europe and Western Asia. Thymus serpyllum f. pseudochamaedrys has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herb for treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Thymus serpyllum f. pseudochamaedrys can be prepared into various herbal products, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to promote overall well-being.
